subject: Statistics
title: The Limits of Prediction
description: Learn The Limits of Prediction in one minute.

hook.text: Philip Tetlock tracked 28,000 expert predictions over 20 years. The average expert barely beat random guessing.

buildup.text: Long-range political and economic predictions are extraordinarily difficult. The world is too complex and nonlinear.

discovery.text: Superforecasters — the best predictors — combine humility, constant updating, and probabilistic thinking.

twist.text: Confident predictions get media attention. Calibrated, uncertain ones are ignored.

climax.text: Knowing the limits of prediction is itself valuable knowledge. Honest uncertainty beats false confidence.

punchline.text: The future is partly unknowable. The best forecasters know what they don't know.

quiz.question: What did Philip Tetlock's research reveal about expert predictions?
options: The average expert barely beat random guessing — but 'superforecasters' with humility performed much better
Long-range predictions are easy for trained professionals
